name: prettier
description: Run prettier formatting check/write on TypeScript/JavaScript/JSON/CSS/MD files. Supports check (verify formatting), write (format in place), and list-unformatted (show only unformatted file names) actions. Automatically handles output limiting and timeout.
when_to_use: Use when you need to check or fix code formatting in TypeScript/JS projects. Replaces 2-3 separate calls to npx prettier with output piping through grep and head. The check action replaces 'npx prettier --check ... 2>&1 | head -N' with a single call. The list-unformatted action replaces 'npx prettier --check ... 2>&1 | grep -E "unformatted|warn" | head -N', which is the most common pattern for checking formatting before committing. The write action replaces 'npx prettier --write ...' with automatic output limiting to prevent token overflow.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'check' (verify formatting, non-zero exit if unformatted), 'write' (format files in place), 'list-unformatted' (show only unformatted file names).

parameters.name: patterns
parameters.description: Glob patterns to match files. Default: 'src/**/*.{ts,js,tsx,jsx,json,css,md}'. Examples: 'src/**/*.ts', 'src/schema/**/*.ts'.

examples.expected: Checks formatting of all TypeScript files under src/ and shows only unformatted file names with 'warn' prefix. Returns exit code 0 if all files are formatted.
Checks formatting of all TypeScript files under src/ and shows up to 20 lines of output. Non-zero exit if any files are unformatted.
Formats the two specified files in place with prettier.
Formats all TypeScript files under src/ with a 120-second timeout.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate steps (cd to project, run npx prettier, pipe through grep for 'unformatted' and head for output limiting) with a single script call. The list-unformatted action specifically replaces the common pattern of 'npx prettier --check ... 2>&1 | grep -E "unformatted|warn" | head -10', which agents frequently use before committing to verify formatting. The write action prevents token overflow from long prettier write output by automatically limiting output lines. The built-in timeout prevents prettier from hanging on large projects.
